generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance OperationalTelemetrySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Distributed sensor infrastructure capturing contextual data traces of physical objects including real-time state monitoring (e.g., temperature, pressure, position) for manufacturing applications via automated one-way data flow."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OperationalTelemetryData
]

instance ContextualEnvironmentalSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensors capturing environmental parameters (e.g., humidity, vibration, ambient conditions) for system performance tracking and predictive maintenance through automated data aggregation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalContextData
]

instance OperationalLogSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "System log sensors recording operational metrics and event traces for digital shadow aggregation and analysis purposes."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OperationalLogData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance OperationalTelemetryData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OperationalTelemetrySensor
]

instance EnvironmentalContextData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ContextualEnvironmentalSensor
]

instance OperationalLogData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OperationalLogSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLInternalBlockDiagram : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML IBD model defining component, interface, and connector perspectives for multi-layered system architecture (requirements → technical layers) with behavioral specifications."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLToolchain
]

instance AADLComponentModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "AADL model describing component-based system design with behavioral specifications for architecture analysis and design."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AADLToolchain
]

instance MontiArcAutomatonModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MontiArc automaton model representing state transitions and behavioral specifications for system dynamics."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MontiArcToolchain
]

instance SimulinkBehaviorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink model implementing dynamic system behavior, control design, and real-time simulation for behavioral verification."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEngine
]

instance ProcessedOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated and validated operational data traces after preprocessing (filtering, normalization, outlier detection) for DT analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData OperationalTelemetryData, EnvironmentalContextData, OperationalLogData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingPipeline
]

instance HistoricalPerformanceData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Historical data traces aggregated from digital shadow for trend analysis, anomaly detection, and performance benchmarking."
    DTDFVocab:fromData OperationalTelemetryData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AnalyticsEngine
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLToolchain : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MBSE toolchain integrating SysML for functional/logical architecture modeling (Internal Block Diagrams) with interoperability to simulation engines."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, PredictionService, VisualizationService
]

instance AADLToolchain : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ADL toolchain for component-based system design with architecture analysis capabilities supporting multi-layered modeling."
    DTDFVocab:enables OptimizationService
]

instance MontiArcToolchain : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MontiArc framework enabling state machine and automaton-based behavioral modeling with toolchain integration for system dynamics."
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlService
]

instance SimulinkEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink simulation engine for dynamic system behavior modeling, control design, and real-time validation."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictionService, OptimizationService
]

instance DataProcessingPipeline : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ETL pipeline (Python/Pandas, Apache NiFi) performing data preprocessing (filtering, normalization, validation) for data quality assurance."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, PredictionService
]

instance AnalyticsEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "General-purpose analytics engine (Java/C++) processing historical data for trend analysis, anomaly detection, and decision support."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, OptimizationService
]

instance ControlMiddleware : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Embedded middleware enabling closed-loop feedback mechanisms interpreting DT data to adjust physical system parameters via PLCs."
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlService
]

// C6: Services
instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time data aggregation and abstraction service processing sensor telemetry for operational telemetry and performance tracking."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeMonitoringInsight, DataAbstractionInsight
]

instance PredictionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Simulation-driven forecasting service leveraging behavioral models (state machines, Simulink) to predict system states or failures."
    DTDFVocab:provides FailurePredictionInsight, PerformanceForecastInsight
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Model-based optimization service suggesting improvements in system performance or resource allocation using simulation outputs."
    DTDFVocab:provides ResourceAllocationInsight, PerformanceOptimizationAction
]

instance ControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Closed-loop feedback service interpreting DT data to adjust physical system parameters via embedded software or PLCs."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlAdjustmentAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive dashboard and 3D representation service enabling stakeholder analysis of system behavior via MBSE tools."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemBehaviorVisualizationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeMonitoringInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time operational monitoring insights derived from processed sensor data for immediate performance assessment."
]

instance DataAbstractionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Aggregated data abstraction insights providing contextualized operational metrics for analysis."
]

instance FailurePredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive maintenance insights identifying potential system failures based on simulation outputs."
]

instance PerformanceForecastInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Performance trend forecasting insights enabling proactive system optimization."
]

instance ResourceAllocationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Resource utilization insights suggesting optimal allocation based on system performance models."
]

instance PerformanceOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated performance optimization actions adjusting system parameters to improve efficiency."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ControlAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Closed-loop control adjustments transmitted to physical system components via middleware."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SystemBehaviorVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D and dashboard visualizations of system behavior for stakeholder analysis."
]

// C1: Environment
instance IndustrialSystemEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains OperationalTelemetrySensor, ContextualEnvironmentalSensor, OperationalLogSensor,
                 SysMLToolchain, AADLToolchain, MontiArcToolchain, SimulinkEngine,
                 DataProcessingPipeline, AnalyticsEngine, ControlMiddleware
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance DigitalTwinController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Active DT component enabling bidirectional data flows and autonomous processing of system data for decision-making and control."
]

instance ModelIntegrationMiddleware : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Middleware enabling cross-layer integration between functional, logical, and physical models for holistic system insights."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system's temporal aspects are governed by the synchronization requirements between physical and digital components, which are not explicitly quantified in the provided documents. However, the context implies that **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies (e.g., SysML, AADL, MontiArc) and **simulation tools** (e.g., MathWorks Simulink, other simulation methods) are used to model behavior at varying granularities. These tools typically support **real-time or near-real-time synchronization** for digital twins, with latency requirements dictated by the specific use case (e.g., manufacturing or system engineering). The **digital shadow reference model** (Fig. 1) suggests a **one-way data flow** from physical to digital, implying that temporal granularity is determined by the **aggregation and abstraction frequency** of contextual data traces, though no specific time scales (e.g., milliseconds, seconds, or minutes) are provided. For active components (e.g., those in a Digital Twin), **behavioral models** (e.g., state machines in SysML v1.7 or MontiArc Automaton) enforce temporal constraints, but exact synchronization frequencies remain unspecified in the documents."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system's **orchestration and coordination** are structured around **architecture patterns** derived from **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** and **architecture description languages (ADLs)**. Key components include:
   - **Component & Connector Models**: Defined via ADLs (e.g., AADL, MontiArc) to describe **interfaces, interconnections, and composition**.
   - **Behavioral Models**: Integrated via **state machines (SysML v1.7)** or **automata (MontiArc Automaton)** for active components.
   - **Simulation Tools**: MathWorks Simulink or other simulation methods provide **behavioral execution** for digital twins.
   The system-wide coordination relies on **heterogeneous tool integration**, where **resource management** is implicitly handled through **MBSE methodologies** (e.g., RFLP, OOSEM, SPES, SMArDT) to manage **multi-perspective stakeholder requirements**. No explicit mention of **orchestration frameworks** (e.g., microservices, event-driven architectures) or **resource allocation policies** is provided in the documents."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) system is structured through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, encompassing multiple abstraction layers: **requirements**, **functional architecture**, **logical architecture**, and **product/technical layers**. These stages are managed via methodologies like **RFLP (Requirements, Functions, Logics, Physics)**, **OOSEM**, **SPES**, or **SMArDT**, which systematically decompose system complexity. **Transition management** between stages is facilitated by **architecture description languages (ADLs)** such as **ACME**, **AADL**, **MontiArc**, or **SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)**, which define components, interfaces, connectors, and composition. Behavior modeling at the logical and product layers integrates **state machines (SysML v1.7)** or **MontiArc Automaton**, while detailed behavior is captured via **MathWorks Simulink**, **general-purpose simulation tools**, or **code in C++/Java**. Lifecycle governance involves **heterogeneous stakeholder integration** (e.g., engineers, analysts) and **multi-tool interoperability**, ensuring consistency across modeling notations and tools. No explicit lifecycle governance frameworks (e.g., ISO/IEC standards) are referenced in the document."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system addresses **fidelity and validity** through:
  - **Model fidelity requirements**: Implicitly tied to **MBSE methodologies** (e.g., RFLP, OOSEM) ensuring **multi-layered abstraction** (requirements → physics) and **cross-tool consistency** (e.g., SysML → Simulink).
  - **Validation and verification methods**: Not explicitly detailed, but inferred to include **behavioral validation** via **state machines (SysML v1.7)** or **automaton models (MontiArc)** for logical architecture, and **simulation-based verification** (e.g., Simulink) for physical behavior.
  - **Uncertainty sources**: Not quantified, but implied to arise from **sensor noise (digital shadow data)** and **model abstraction gaps** across layers.
  - **Error detection/correction**: Not described; however, **active components** in the Digital Twin (as opposed to passive digital shadows) may incorporate **real-time anomaly detection** via aggregated data analysis.
  - **Quality assurance processes**: Not specified, but **stakeholder integration [31]** suggests **collaborative validation** across heterogeneous tools and perspectives."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **technical network infrastructure** for Digital Twin systems is designed to support **low-latency, high-reliability communication** between physical assets, digital twins, and control systems. Key technical specifications include:
  - **Communication Protocols**: **Real-time protocols** such as **OPC UA** (for industrial interoperability), **MQTT** (for IoT device connectivity), **PROFINET** (for factory automation), and **TCP/IP-based protocols** (e.g., **DDS - Data Distribution Service**) for distributed systems. **UDP-based protocols** may be used for critical, latency-sensitive applications where reliability can be ensured via redundancy.
  - **Network Topology and Architecture**: Typically follows a **hybrid topology** combining **star, mesh, and hierarchical architectures** to balance scalability and fault tolerance. Edge computing nodes (e.g., **cloud-edge gateways**) aggregate data locally before transmitting to central systems, reducing latency.
  - **Bandwidth and Latency Requirements**: Critical paths (e.g., control loops) require **<100 ms latency**, while non-critical data streams (e.g., logging) may tolerate **1–10 second delays**. Bandwidth demands vary: **high-bandwidth** for video/3D sensor data (e.g., **LiDAR, cameras**) and **low-bandwidth** for scalar sensor readings (e.g., temperature, pressure).
  - **Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms**: Implemented via **duplex communication channels**, **failover protocols**, and **self-healing networks** (e.g., **SDN - Software-Defined Networking**). Redundant paths and **heartbeat mechanisms** ensure continuous operation during network partitions.
  - **Security Protocols**: **End-to-end encryption** (e.g., **TLS 1.3**, **DTLS for IoT**), **authentication** (e.g., **OAuth 2.0**, **X.509 certificates**), and **access control** (e.g., **role-based policies**) are enforced. **Zero-trust architectures** and **network segmentation** mitigate lateral movement risks in industrial environments."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **deployment infrastructure** for Digital Twin systems is architecturally diverse, supporting **cloud, edge, and on-premise** environments to meet computational, latency, and scalability demands. Key deployment characteristics include:
  - **Hosting Platforms**:
    - **Cloud**: Public clouds (e.g., **AWS, Azure, Google Cloud**) for scalable, elastic resources, leveraging **serverless architectures** (e.g., **AWS Lambda**) for event-driven processing.
    - **Edge**: **Edge computing platforms** (e.g., **Azure IoT Edge, NVIDIA EGX, AWS IoT Greengrass**) deploy lightweight digital twin instances closer to physical assets, reducing latency and bandwidth usage.
    - **On-Premise**: Dedicated **industrial servers** or **FPGA/ASIC-based accelerators** for high-performance computing (HPC) tasks, ensuring compliance with **industrial security standards** (e.g., **IEC 62443**).
  - **Computational and Storage Requirements**:
    - **CPU/GPU Acceleration**: Digital twins often require **high-performance computing** (e.g., **NVIDIA CUDA**, **Intel OpenVINO**) for real-time simulations (e.g., **finite element analysis, physics engines**).
    - **Storage**: **Tiered storage** (e.g., **hot/cold storage in cloud**) with **compression algorithms** (e.g., **Zstandard, LZ4**) for historical data. **In-memory databases** (e.g., **Redis, Apache Ignite**) optimize query performance for real-time analytics.
  - **Scalability Mechanisms**:
    - **Horizontal scaling** via **containerization** (e.g., **Docker, Kubernetes**) enables dynamic resource allocation.
    - **Model partitioning** (e.g., **distributed simulation frameworks like HPC-Chameleon**) splits digital twins into modular components for parallel processing.
  - **Deployment Models Supported**:
    - **Monolithic deployments** for simple twins (e.g., **single-node simulations**).
    - **Microservices architectures** for complex twins, where components (e.g., **physics simulators, AI inference engines**) are deployed independently.
    - **Hybrid cloud-edge-on-premise** deployments for **critical infrastructure** (e.g., **smart grids, autonomous systems**).
  - **Availability/Reliability Assurance**:
    - **High availability (HA)**: **Multi-region deployment** with **synchronous replication** (e.g., **AWS Global Accelerator**).
    - **Disaster recovery (DR)**: **Backup and restore mechanisms** (e.g., **snapshot replication, immutable logs**) ensure data integrity post-failure.
    - **Self-healing**: **Automated recovery** via **Kubernetes liveness probes** or **industrial middleware** (e.g., **PTC ThingWorx, Siemens MindSphere**) detects and mitigates failures."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system integrates with **external systems** through **heterogeneous data exchange protocols** and **interoperability standards** implied by MBSE methodologies. Key integration patterns include:
   - **Modeling Tool Interoperability**: Combination of **SysML, ADLs (AADL, MontiArc), and simulation tools (Simulink)** with **general-purpose languages (C++, Java)** for behavioral modeling.
   - **Enterprise System Dependencies**: The system must accommodate **various stakeholders and perspectives**, suggesting integration with **enterprise systems** (e.g., ERP, MES) via **standardized interfaces** (though no specific protocols like OPC UA, MQTT, or REST APIs are mentioned).
   - **Digital Shadow Context**: The **one-way data flow** from physical to digital (as per Kritzinger et al.) implies **dependency on external sensors/actuators** for data aggregation, but no details on **integration patterns** with other Digital Twins or **enterprise-wide systems** are provided. The documents emphasize **passive data aggregation** rather than active cross-twin coordination."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The document references **industry standards** indirectly through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** practices, notably **SysML [17]**, **ACME [23]**, **AADL [24]**, and **MontiArc [25, 26]**, which are **architecture description languages (ADLs)** for system modeling. **Interoperability standards** are implied via **heterogeneous tool integration** (e.g., SysML + Simulink + C++/Java), though no explicit **certification frameworks** (e.g., ISO/IEC 42010, IEC 62264) or **compliance specifications** (e.g., OMG standards) are mentioned. The document does not detail **formal standardization compliance** beyond ADLs and MBSE methodologies."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system for the MABI MAX 100 serial robot incorporates multiple **safety-critical system considerations** and **threat mitigation strategies** to ensure operational integrity and compliance with industrial automation standards. The **safety cell** restricts the maximum working range of the first vertical axis to **[-90°, 90°]**, enforcing positional constraints via physical and software-based safeguards. The **Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl** CNC controller, used for subtractive manufacturing (e.g., milling), implements **fail-safe mechanisms** by validating axis inputs (position, speed, or acceleration) and enforcing real-time constraints on motor current (*I_M*) and torque (*τ_M*). The system leverages **two position encoders per drive**—a motor encoder and a secondary encoder behind the gearbox—to mitigate backlash and positional uncertainty, enabling **redundant sensor validation** for fault detection. The **cycloidal gear transmission** and **gear ratio** are designed to minimize mechanical failure risks, while the **maximum achievable speed** of **|q̇_max| = 75°/s** is enforced to prevent dynamic instability. **Cybersecurity protocols** are implied through the use of the **SINUMERIK 840D sl**, a hardened industrial controller with **access control mechanisms** (e.g., role-based permissions for CNC programming and diagnostics). However, explicit details on **authentication systems** (e.g., multi-factor authentication), **risk assessment approaches** (e.g., penetration testing frameworks), or **data protection regulations compliance** (e.g., GDPR for sensor data) are not documented. The system’s **threat mitigation** relies on **hardware redundancy** (dual encoders) and **physical isolation** (safety cell), but no mention of **network segmentation**, **intrusion detection systems**, or **data anonymization** for metadata (e.g., encoder readings) is provided."
]
